title: TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH

provenance.section: TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH
provenance.neighboringText: TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH FROM INCORRECT GROUNDING WIRE CONNECTION: Check with a qualified electrician if you are in doubt as to whether the outlet is properly grounded. Do not use the welder if the power cord or plug is damaged. If damaged, have it repaired by a service facility before us

TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH FROM INCORRECT GROUNDING WIRE CONNECTION: Check with a qualified electrician if you are in doubt as to whether the outlet is properly grounded. Do not use the welder if the power cord or plug is damaged. If damaged, have it repaired by a service facility before us
Extension Cords
Do not use an extension cord on this welder. Replacement Cords

title: 10.	Screw the Spool Knob into the Spool Adapter.

provenance.section: 10.	Screw the Spool Knob into the Spool Adapter.
provenance.neighboringText: 10. Screw the Spool Knob into the Spool Adapter.

title: 11.	 Turn the Feed Tensioner knob counterclockwise to

provenance.section: 11.	 Turn the Feed Tensioner knob counterclockwise to
provenance.neighboringText: 11. Turn the Feed Tensioner knob counterclockwise to loosen it enough to pull it down to remove tension. The spring-loaded Idler Arm will move up as shown.

title: 13.	Loosen the Knob on the Wire Feed

provenance.section: 13.	Loosen the Knob on the Wire Feed
provenance.neighboringText: 13. Loosen the Knob on the Wire Feed mechanism, then insert the Gun Cable Connector through the hole on the Welder front and into the socket on the Wire Feed.

title: 14.	Ensure that the Gun Cable Connector

provenance.section: 14.	Ensure that the Gun Cable Connector
provenance.neighboringText: 14. Ensure that the Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into the socket on the Wire Feed mechanism as shown, then tighten the Knob securely. If Connector is not fully inserted, the gas connection will leak, preventing shielding gas from reaching the welding arc.

title: 15.	Insert the Wire Feed Control Cable through

provenance.section: 15.	Insert the Wire Feed Control Cable through
provenance.neighboringText: 15. Insert the Wire Feed Control Cable through the hole on the Welder front and connect it to the Wire Feed Control Socket inside the machine, then tighten the lock ring on the Cable plug. Note that the plug on the Cable fits into the Socket in one specific orientation only.

title: 18.	Cut off all bent and crimped wire.

provenance.section: 18.	Cut off all bent and crimped wire.
provenance.neighboringText: 18. Cut off all bent and crimped wire. The cut end must have no burrs or sharp edges; cut again if needed.

title: 19.	Keep tension on the wire and guide at

provenance.section: 19.	Keep tension on the wire and guide at
provenance.neighboringText: 19. Keep tension on the wire and guide at least 12 inches of wire into the Wire Inlet Liner and Feed Guide.

title: 20.	Make sure the welding wire is resting in the groove

provenance.section: 20.	Make sure the welding wire is resting in the groove
provenance.neighboringText: 20. Make sure the welding wire is resting in the groove of the Feed Roller, then push the wire Idler Arm down, and swing the Feed Tensioner up to latch it across the tip of the arm. After the wire is held by the Tensioner, you may release it.

title: 21.	Pull the Nozzle to remove it.

provenance.section: 21.	Pull the Nozzle to remove it.
provenance.neighboringText: 21. Pull the Nozzle to remove it.
22. Unscrew the Contact Tip counterclockwise and remove.

title: 23.	Lay the MIG Gun Cable out in a straight line

provenance.section: 23.	Lay the MIG Gun Cable out in a straight line
provenance.neighboringText: 23. Lay the MIG Gun Cable out in a straight line so that the welding wire moves through it easily. Leave the cover open, so that the feed mechanism can be observed.

title: PARTS MAY BE AT WELDING VOLTAGE

provenance.section: PARTS MAY BE AT WELDING VOLTAGE
provenance.neighboringText: PARTS MAY BE AT WELDING VOLTAGE TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH:

title: TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH:

provenance.section: TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH:
provenance.neighboringText: PARTS MAY BE AT WELDING VOLTAGE TO PREVENT ELECTRIC SHOCK AND DEATH:
1. Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism.

title: Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism.

provenance.section: Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism.
provenance.neighboringText: 1. Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism.
2. Close door before plugging in, unless using Cold Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun.

title: Close door before plugging in, unless using Cold

provenance.section: Close door before plugging in, unless using Cold
provenance.neighboringText: 2. Close door before plugging in, unless using Cold Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun.

title: Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun.

provenance.section: Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun.
provenance.neighboringText: 2. Close door before plugging in, unless using Cold Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun.
3. Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun.

title: Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun.

provenance.section: Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun.
provenance.neighboringText: 3. Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun.

title: 24.	Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power

provenance.section: 24.	Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power
provenance.neighboringText: 24. Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power Cord into Power Input Socket.
24. Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power Cord into Power Input Socket.
Note: Plug will only fit one way.

title: Plug will only fit one way.

provenance.section: Plug will only fit one way.
provenance.neighboringText: Note: Plug will only fit one way.

title: 25.	Do not touch the Gun’s Trigger.  Plug the Power

provenance.section: 25.	Do not touch the Gun’s Trigger.  Plug the Power
provenance.neighboringText: 25. Do not touch the Gun’s Trigger. Plug the Power Cord into a properly grounded, GFCI protected 120 VAC (20 amp rated) or 240 VAC receptacle that matches the plug and turn the Power Switch ON. The circuit must be equipped with delayed action-type circuit breaker or fuses.

title: Cord into a properly grounded, GFCI protected

provenance.section: Cord into a properly grounded, GFCI protected
provenance.neighboringText: 25. Do not touch the Gun’s Trigger. The circuit must be equipped with delayed action-type circuit breaker or fuses.

title: If the wire does not feed

provenance.section: If the wire does not feed
provenance.neighboringText: If the wire does not feed properly and the Spool is stationary, turn OFF and unplug the Welder and slightly tighten the Feed Tensioner clockwise before retrying.

title: 27.	To check the wire’s drive tension, press and hold

provenance.section: 27.	To check the wire’s drive tension, press and hold
provenance.neighboringText: 27. To check the wire’s drive tension, press and hold Trigger to feed the wire against a piece of wood from 2 to 3 inches away. Note: After pressing Trigger, wire will stop feeding after 3 seconds if there is no arc. Check tension for less than 3 seconds. If the wire stops instead of bending, unplug

title: 28.	Turn OFF the Power Switch and unplug the

provenance.section: 28.	Turn OFF the Power Switch and unplug the
provenance.neighboringText: 28. Turn OFF the Power Switch and unplug the Power Cord from its electrical outlet.
29. Close the Door. Make sure Door is securely latched.

title: 30.	Select a Contact Tip that is compatible with

provenance.section: 30.	Select a Contact Tip that is compatible with
provenance.neighboringText: 30. Select a Contact Tip that is compatible with the welding wire used. Slide the Contact Tip over the wire and thread it clockwise into the MIG Gun. Tighten the Contact Tip.

provenance.neighboringText: 31. Replace the Nozzle and cut the wire off at 1/2" from tip (1/2" stickout).

title: 2. Use clamps (not included) to hold the workpieces

provenance.section: 2. Use clamps (not included) to hold the workpieces
provenance.neighboringText: 2. Use clamps (not included) to hold the workpieces in position so that you can concentrate on proper welding technique. The distance (if any) between the two workpieces must be controlled properly to allow the weld to hold both sides securely while allowing the weld to penetrate fully into the join

title: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY AND DEATH:

provenance.section: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY AND DEATH:
provenance.neighboringText: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY AND DEATH: Do not weld without Grounding Clamp. When the operator is not holding the Torch, it must be sitting on a nonconductive, nonflammable surface. Only hold TIG Rod with an electrically insulated welding glove.

TO PREVENT DEATH FROM ASPHYXIATION: Do not open gas without proper ventilation. Fix gas leaks immediately. Shielding gas can displace air and cause rapid loss of consciousness and death. Shielding gas without carbon dioxide can be even more hazardous because asphyxiation can start without feeling sh
WARNING! TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY: Welder is now energized and Open Circuit Voltage is present.

title: 10.	Hold TIG Torch in one gloved hand and the TIG

provenance.section: 10.	Hold TIG Torch in one gloved hand and the TIG
provenance.neighboringText: 10. Hold TIG Torch in one gloved hand and the TIG Rod (sold separately) in other gloved hand.

title: 11.	 Open valve on TIG Torch to start gas flow.

provenance.section: 11.	 Open valve on TIG Torch to start gas flow.
provenance.neighboringText: 11. Open valve on TIG Torch to start gas flow.

title: 12.	To initiate welding arc, push Foot Pedal and touch

provenance.section: 12.	To initiate welding arc, push Foot Pedal and touch
provenance.neighboringText: 12. To initiate welding arc, push Foot Pedal and touch Electrode to work piece and lift. If not using Foot Pedal, touch Electrode to work piece and lift.

title: 13.	When welding puddle is hot enough, tilt Torch

provenance.section: 13.	When welding puddle is hot enough, tilt Torch
provenance.neighboringText: 13. When welding puddle is hot enough, tilt Torch backward about 10-15 degrees from vertical and move it back slightly. Add TIG Rod material as needed to the front end of the weld puddle.
14. Alternate between pushing the torch/weld puddle and adding the TIG Rod material.

title: 15.	When finished welding, release the Foot Pedal but

provenance.section: 15.	When finished welding, release the Foot Pedal but
provenance.neighboringText: 15. When finished welding, release the Foot Pedal but keep Torch on weld puddle until weld solidifies. If not using Foot Pedal, pull Torch away from work piece until welding arc is broken, then return the gas coverage until weld solidifies.

title: 16.	Close valve on TIG Torch and turn Right Knob

provenance.section: 16.	Close valve on TIG Torch and turn Right Knob
provenance.neighboringText: 16. Close valve on TIG Torch and turn Right Knob to OFF to turn off power to TIG Torch.

title: 17.	Set TIG Torch down on nonconductive,

provenance.section: 17.	Set TIG Torch down on nonconductive,
provenance.neighboringText: 17. Set TIG Torch down on nonconductive, nonflammable surface away from any grounded objects.
18. Turn the Power Switch OFF.
19. To prevent accidents, after use:

Wire Weld Penetration (Workpiece Heat Control)
EXCESS PENETRATION OR
BURN-THROUGH Weld droops on top and underneath, or falls through
PROPER PENETRATION Weld is visible underneath and
INADEQUATE PENETRATION

POSSIBLE CAUSES AND SOLUTIONS 1. Workpiece overheating: Reduce wire feed speed. Decrease weld current. 2. Travel speed too slow: Increase travel speed and ensure that travel speed is kept steady. 3. Excessive material at weld: Reduce wire feed speed.

Wire Weld – Burn-Through

POSSIBLE CAUSES AND SOLUTIONS 1. Workpiece overheating: Reduce current and/or wire feed speed. 2. Travel speed too slow: Increase travel speed and ensure that travel speed is kept steady. 3. Excessive material at weld: Reduce wire feed speed.

title: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS:

provenance.section: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS:
provenance.neighboringText: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY, FIRE AND BURNS: Unplug the Welder, rest the tool on a heat-proof, electrically non-conductive surface, and allow all parts of the Welder to cool thoroughly before service.
1. BEFORE EACH USE, inspect the general condition of the Welder. Check for:
2. PERIODICALLY, have a qualified technician remove the Rear Panel and use compressed air to blow out all dust from the interior.
3. AFTER EVERY USE , store in a clean and dry location.
